placeholder.events: Filter events by path or name...

help.title: Search & Filter Guide
help.headerLine: Quick syntax reference for the files search box.
help.subtitle: Cardinal can match file paths, folder paths, filenames, folder names, and file contents using plain terms, quoted phrases, wildcards, operators, and filters.
help.examplesDescription: Each example shows one syntax feature. Click any example to add it to the files search box

exampleDescriptions.plainWord: A space means AND, so this matches file paths or folder paths containing both test and report
exampleDescriptions.quotedPhrase: Matches file paths or folder paths containing the exact phrase Application Support, including the space
exampleDescriptions.extensionWildcard: Matches files whose filename ends with .sqlite
exampleDescriptions.extensionFilter: Matches files whose filename extension is pdf
exampleDescriptions.typeFilter: Matches files in the picture category
exampleDescriptions.typeMacro: Matches files in the audio category using the audio: shortcut
exampleDescriptions.globstarPath: Matches files named Cargo.toml anywhere under a src folder tree
exampleDescriptions.todayModified: Matches files or folders modified today
exampleDescriptions.sizeRange: Matches files whose size is between 1 MB and 10 MB
exampleDescriptions.regex: Uses a regular expression to match a file or folder name segment named README.md exactly
exampleDescriptions.content: Matches files whose contents contain the word TODO
exampleDescriptions.noSubfoldersScope: Matches the folder itself plus direct file children under /Users/demo/Projects, without descending into nested subfolders

files.title: Files tab syntax
files.description: Use the main search box to match file paths, folder paths, filenames, folder names, and file contents with flexible syntax.
files.fullPath: Plain search terms are checked against the full indexed file path or folder path, not just the filename.
files.words: A plain word like report matches any file path or folder path containing that text.
files.phrases: A quoted phrase like "Application Support" matches file paths or folder paths containing that exact text, including spaces.
files.wildcards: In file and folder path matching, '*' matches zero or more characters, '?' matches exactly one character, and '**' crosses folder levels when used between slashes.
files.globstar: '**' crosses folder levels in file or folder paths when used as its own path segment, like src/**/Cargo.toml.
files.literalWildcards: Quote a token like "*.rs" if you want the literal characters '*' or '?' matched in a file path or folder path.
files.pathSegments: Use '/' inside a term to match file or folder path segments, prefixes, suffixes, or exact folder names.
files.segmentAnchors: In file or folder path matching, /foo is a segment prefix, foo/ is a segment suffix, and /foo/ matches an exact folder name.
files.implicitAnd: A space means both terms must appear somewhere in the matched file path or folder path.
files.orOperator: Use OR or '|' when either term can appear in the matched file path or folder path.
files.notOperator: Use NOT or a leading '!' to exclude files or folders whose path matches that term.
files.grouping: Use parentheses '(...)' or angle brackets '<...>' to group file or folder path conditions and filters together.
files.precedence: When you combine file or folder path conditions, NOT is applied first, OR next, and AND last.
files.caseSensitivity: The Aa toggle changes case-sensitive matching for file and folder path text, content: text, and regex: patterns.
files.expandOnTilde: The '~' character expands to your home folder.

filters.title: Common filters
filters.description: These are some of the most useful filters you can combine with normal search terms.
filters.fileFolder: file: keeps only file results, and folder: keeps only folder results.
filters.extensions: ext: matches files by filename extension, like ext:jpg;png.
filters.types: type: matches files whose extension belongs to a category like picture, code, or archive.
filters.typeMacros: audio:, video:, doc:, and exe: are shortcuts for common type: categories, and each can take an optional argument.
filters.dates: dm: and dc: filter modified and created timestamps using keywords, absolute dates, ranges, or comparisons.
filters.size: size: filters by file size using comparisons, ranges, or keywords like tiny and empty.
filters.scope: parent:, in:/infolder:, and nosubfolders: restrict results under an absolute folder path.
filters.regexContent: regex: matches a regular expression against a file or folder name segment, and content: matches text inside file contents.

tooltips.kind: Choose whether results should be files only or folders only
tooltips.type: Insert a type: filter such as picture, audio, code, or archive
tooltips.extension: Merge selected file extensions into one ext: filter, like ext:pdf;md
tooltips.date: Insert a modified/created date filter such as dm:thisweek or dc:today
tooltips.size: Insert a size: filter such as size:huge or size:1mb..10mb
tooltips.regexExample: Append a sample regex filter: regex:^README\.md$

scope.in: Pick a folder and search anywhere under that folder
scope.parent: Pick a folder and limit results to its direct children
scope.noSubfolders: Pick a folder and keep only that folder plus its direct file children

stateDisplay.emptyMessage: Try adjusting your keywords or filters.

sorting.disabled: Sorting is disabled. Too many results ({{limit}}+) may impact performance. You can adjust the threshold in Preferences.

empty.title: No recent file events yet.
empty.hint: Keep working and check back for updates.

rescan.disabledHint: Rescan becomes available once Cardinal is ready.
rescan.errorCountSuffix: {{formatted}} filesystem errors have occurred, rescan is suggested.

fullDiskAccess.title: Full Disk Access Required
fullDiskAccess.description: Cardinal needs macOS Full Disk Access to index and monitor files across your system. Please enable Full Disk Access for Cardinal in System Settings.

steps.one: Open System Settings > Privacy & Security > Full Disk Access.
steps.two: Click the lock to make changes and enable Cardinal.
steps.three: When prompted, allow macOS to restart Cardinal.

preferences.title: Preferences
preferences.heading: Make Cardinal yours
preferences.subtitle: Adjust appearance and language to your liking.
preferences.themeHint: Choose light, dark, or follow the system.

watchRoot.label: Monitor root path
watchRoot.help: Changing this will rebuild the index for the new directory.

errors.absolute: Please enter an absolute path starting with '/' or '~'.

watchRoot.title: Choose monitor root path
watchRoot.description: Cardinal will index and monitor files under this directory. You can change it later in Preferences.

ignorePaths.label: Ignore paths
ignorePaths.help: One path per line; ignored paths are skipped during indexing.

errors.absolute: Each path must start with '/' or '~'.
